items.question: Which sentence is PASSIVE?
options: The bone was buried by the dog.
The dog is burying the bone.
explanations: This is active — the dog does the action.
Correct! 'was buried' = be + participle, and the bone receives the action.
This is active continuous.
This is active present simple.

items.question: Make it passive: 'The gardener waters the plants.'
options: The plants are watered by the gardener.
The plants watered by the gardener.
The plants is watered by the gardener.
explanations: Correct! Present passive: 'The plants are watered by the gardener'.
This reverses the meaning.
This is missing 'are' — no passive without 'be'.
'Plants' is plural, so use 'are', not 'is'.

items.question: Make it active: 'The email was sent by Maria.'
explanations: Correct! The doer (Maria) becomes the subject: 'Maria sent the email'.
This reverses the meaning.
This changes the meaning — Maria becomes the receiver.
This changes the tense to present continuous.

items.question: Which is the better choice: 'Shakespeare wrote Hamlet.'?
options: Active — the doer matters and it's clear.
explanations: Correct! Naming Shakespeare matters, so the active is clearer and stronger.
The doer clearly matters here, so it isn't unimportant.
The doer is known (Shakespeare), so this doesn't apply.
There is a difference: the active is the better fit here.

items.question: Best sentence when you DON'T know who did it:
explanations: 'Someone stole' works, but the passive keeps the focus on the bike.
Correct! The passive is natural when the doer is unknown: 'My bike was stolen'.
'Is stealing' is active continuous and makes no sense here.
'My bike stole' means the bike did the stealing.

items.question: Which sentence is ACTIVE?
options: The results will be published soon.
explanations: This is passive (was + cleaned).
This is passive (will be + published).
Correct! 'The council approved the plan' — the subject does the action.
This is passive (is + grown).

items.question: Rewrite more clearly: 'It was decided by us to cancel the trip.'
options: We decided to cancel the trip.
The trip was decided to be cancelled by us.
It was us who the trip was cancelled by.
explanations: Correct! The active is far clearer: 'We decided to cancel the trip'.
This is even more clumsy and passive.
This is still vague and passive.
This is awkward and ungrammatical.

items.question: Make it passive (doer unimportant): 'They serve breakfast until 10.'
explanations: Correct! Present passive, doer dropped: 'Breakfast is served until 10'.
'Serves' is active; breakfast doesn't serve.
'Is serving' is active continuous.
'Was served' changes it to the past.

items.question: Which sentence keeps the topic 'penicillin' in focus?
options: Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming.
A discovery was made by Fleming.
explanations: This is fine, but it starts with the doer, not the topic.
Correct! The passive keeps 'penicillin' as the subject/topic.
This starts with Fleming, shifting the focus.
This starts with 'a discovery', not penicillin.

items.question: When is the ACTIVE voice usually the right default?
options: When you want clear, direct writing naming the doer.
When you want to hide who did it.
When the verb has no object.
explanations: Correct! The active is the clear, direct default that names the doer.
An unknown doer is a reason to use the passive.
Hiding the doer is a reason to use the passive.
A verb with no object simply can't be passive anyway.
